content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array and he also likes beautiful things.
So he wants to make his array as beautiful as possible.

He defines the BeautyBeautyBeauty of his array as the sum of all pairwise products of the elements of the array.

Beauty([A1,A2,‚Ä¶,AN])=‚ąĎi=1N‚ąĎj=i+1NA[i]‚čÖA[j]Beauty([A_1, A_2, \dots, A_N]) = \displaystyle\sum\limits_{i=1}^N\displaystyle\sum\limits_{j=i+1}^N A[i] \cdot A[j]Beauty([A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč])=i=1‚ąĎN‚Äčj=i+1‚ąĎN‚ÄčA[i]‚čÖA[j]

To make his array more beautiful, he can do the following operation at most KKK times:

In a single operation, he can increase any element of the array by 111. That is, he can select any iii, 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚Č§i‚Č§N, and change AiA_iAi‚Äč to Ai+1A_i + 1Ai‚Äč+1.

Given the original array that Chef has, and KKK, find the maximum BeautyBeautyBeauty that he can achieve after applying at most KKK operations. Since the answer might be large, output it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each testcase contains two integers NNN and KKK, the size of the array and the maximum number of operations possible.
The next line contains NNN integers, A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.


Output Format
For each testcase, output one integer in a new line, the maximum BeautyBeautyBeauty of the array achievable, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all testcases is ‚Č§5‚čÖ105\leq 5 \cdot 10^5‚Č§5‚čÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Testcase 1: The array given is [4,1][4, 1][4,1]. You can use at most 222 operations. Maximum beauty is achieved when 222 operations are applied on A2A_2A2‚Äč. This changes the array to [4,3][4, 3][4,3], which has a BeautyBeautyBeauty of 4‚ąó3=124 * 3 = 124‚ąó3=12. You can show that this is the best that you can do. Hence, the answer is 121212.
Testcase 2: Since K=0K = 0K=0, no operations ar possible, and the input array is the final array as well. So the final BeautyBeautyBeauty is 5‚ąó6=305 * 6 = 305‚ąó6=30.
Testcase 3: The array given is [7,1,3][7, 1, 3][7,1,3]. You can use at most 333 operations. One way to achieve maximum beauty is by applying the first 222 operations on A2A_2A2‚Äč, and the last operation on A3A_3A3‚Äč. This changes the array to [7,3,4][7, 3, 4][7,3,4], which has a BeautyBeautyBeauty of (7‚ąó3)+(7‚ąó4)+(3‚ąó4)=21+28+12=61(7 * 3) + (7 * 4) + (3 * 4) = 21 + 28 + 12 = 61(7‚ąó3)+(7‚ąó4)+(3‚ąó4)=21+28+12=61. You can show that this is the best that you can do. Hence, the answer is 616161.
